title: Initiative Overview
subtitle: Research Infrastructure and Collaboration Opportunity
summary: A collaboration-facing overview of the partnerships, program lanes, and institutional value around Sutton SignWriting infrastructure.
synopsis: This deck is the collaboration and program invitation for the initiative. It explains why institutional partnership is timely, how Brazil strengthens the empirical case, how South Korea strengthens the current institutional proof case, what concrete lanes of work are now visible, and what a PI, lab, or host institution could help build.

slides.title: Why This Initiative Exists
slides.summary: The initiative exists to connect a globally used but informally maintained infrastructure system to durable collaboration, research, and institutional support.
bullets: Globally used writing and technical ecosystem
Infrastructure maintained through an informal support model
slides.speech: This initiative exists because Sutton SignWriting has reached a different stage of development. It is already a globally used writing and technical ecosystem, but it is still maintained through a support model that is too informal for its current value. The central question is no longer whether SignWriting matters. The question is what kind of collaboration and institutional support can stabilize, extend, and study what already exists.
slides.notes: Open with the initiative logic rather than redoing a full definition of SignWriting.

slides.title: Why Partnership Matters Now
slides.summary: The need is timely because the infrastructure is important, the maintenance model is fragile, and the evidence base is stronger than many institutional audiences realize.
bullets: Brazil gives the case a documented empirical center
South Korea gives the case a current official proof point
Independent maintenance is no longer enough
slides.speech: The timing matters because the infrastructure is already important enough that fragility now has visible consequences. A recent outage disrupted educational programs in Brazil, which is especially significant because Brazil is now the clearest documented empirical center in the public record. South Korea adds a different kind of evidence by showing that SignWriting already appears inside current official dictionary infrastructure. The issue is not only technical vulnerability. It is that the work has outgrown a purely independent maintenance model.
slides.notes: Use the outage and Brazil together as the clearest urgency argument, then add South Korea as the current official proof point.

slides.title: Program Lanes
slides.summary: The initiative now has several concrete lanes of work that a PI, lab, or institutional partner could help organize.
bullets: Korea-grounded institutional documentation and partnership work
Translation and access to especially important works
slides.speech: The work is now concrete enough to describe as a program rather than only a general appeal. The clearest lanes are reading and literacy research, Brazil-grounded institutional and materials work, Korea-grounded institutional documentation and partnership work, corpus and dataset development, and translation-and-access work around especially important international materials. That gives collaborators something specific to join, shape, and fund.
slides.notes: This is the program-structure slide. Make the lanes feel concrete.

slides.title: Collaboration Focus
slides.summary: Institutional participation can take several practical forms across funding, research, staffing, and access work.
slides.speech: Collaboration can take several practical forms. It can include grant development through NIH, NSF, NEH, and international proposals; administered giving and bridge support where appropriate; research integration in literacy, cognition, and AI; rights-aware translation and access for especially important international works; and institutional alignment through existing labs, staffing structures, and collaboration models.
slides.notes: Show that the work is practical, not abstract.

slides.title: Platform Development and Institutional Value
slides.summary: Platform V3, the Brazil evidence base, the Korea proof point, and international use make this a strong institutional opportunity.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for platform development and institutional value.
bullets: Structured registration for students, teachers, and researchers
Support for institutional and educational use
Foundation for AI and translation pipelines
slides.speech: SignWriting Platform V3 is intended to provide structured user registration, anonymized demographic data for research, support for institutional and educational use, and a foundation for AI and translation pipelines. Together with the stronger documented Brazil evidence base and the current Korean institutional proof point, that gives institutions access to a globally used linguistic system with strong research and accessibility value.
slides.notes: This is the future-facing systems slide.

slides.title: Call to Collaborate
slides.summary: The initiative is seeking faculty collaborators and institutional alignment.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for call to collaborate.
slides.speech: We are seeking faculty interested in serving as PI or co-investigator, developing grant proposals, helping shape administered giving pathways through the University of Iowa where appropriate, and integrating SignWriting into research programs.
